title: Aureline publish-later queue, queue-review, consequence-preview, account-mapping, and provider-object-relation vocabulary
description: Boundary schema for the publish_later_queue_item_record, publish_later_queue_review_record, provider_consequence_preview_record, account_mapping_binding_record, and provider_object_relation_record frozen in docs/providers/provider_mode_contract.md. The contract keeps a provider-linked action alive across offline, provider outage, browser-handoff blocked, status-preview, account-switch, freshness-drift, policy-epoch-roll, and rollback transitions without losing origin, scope, or next-safe-action truth. The frozen relation set (local_draft, queued_publish, browser_handoff, provider_authoritative_object, cached_read_only_shadow) is the only vocabulary provider-linked surfaces render. Raw URLs, raw callback bodies, raw webhook bodies, raw delegated-token bodies, raw provider payloads, and raw preview bodies never cross this boundary; records carry opaque refs, fingerprints, and structured fields only. The eventual provider-mode crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary every non-owning surface reads. Adding a new mode_admission_reason, prerequisite_class, queue_state, conflict_policy_class, reopen_semantic, review_disposition_class, resolution_state_class, relation_class, drift_state_class, next_safe_action_class, or irreversibility_class is additive-minor and requires a publish_later_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

publish_later_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this publish-later vocabulary export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / queue-review rollups / admin-reconciliation consoles. Raw URLs, raw tokens, raw callback bodies, and raw provider payloads never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short human-readable sentence safe to show in preview, queue-review, and export review surfaces.

provider_actor_class.description: Provider-actor class reused from schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json. A generic 'Connected' state is forbidden on every product, CLI, support-export, queue-review, and audit surface. 'unknown_actor_class' is repair-only and MUST NOT be collapsed with any other class.

reviewer_actor_class.description: Actor class allowed to author a queue-review entry. Extends the ADR-0010 actor set with admin_reviewer for admin-console-led reviews.

trust_posture.description: ADR-0001 workspace-trust state the queue item, mapping, preview, or relation was captured under.

redaction_class.description: Declared redaction class reused from ADR-0010 and ADR-0007 redaction posture. Raw URLs, raw callback bodies, raw webhook bodies, raw delegated-token bodies, raw provider payloads, and raw preview bodies never cross this boundary regardless of class.

mutation_mode.description: Mutation mode reused from ADR-0010. Mode is disclosed at the point of intent; publish_now and local_draft MAY NOT collapse into a single control. A surface that cannot name a mode MUST route to inspect_only rather than default to publish_now.

surface_class.description: Frozen provider-linked surface class that minted the queue item or relation.

mode_admission_reason.description: Why the queue item was admitted into the publish-later queue instead of publishing immediately. Every item names exactly one.

prerequisite_class.description: Typed prerequisite that must be satisfied before a queue item is admitted for drain.

prerequisite_state.description: State of one prerequisite. Drain authority is revalidated at drain: if any required prerequisite has regressed from met to unmet, drain fails closed.

linked_record_ref.description: Opaque ref to the record that will clear the prerequisite (health probe record, account mapping binding, approval-ticket record, preview record).

allOf.description: cleared_at MUST be present when prerequisite_state is met.

conflict_policy_class.description: Conflict policy for the queue item. force_overwrite_with_preview MUST carry a consequence_preview_ref.

reopen_semantic.description: Condition under which a parked queue item becomes eligible for re-evaluation. Every item names at least one; multiple are allowed for compound prerequisites.

queue_state.description: Queue state rendered explicitly on queue-review, support-export, and admin-reconciliation surfaces.

review_disposition_class.description: Typed queue-review disposition. Every queue-review entry names exactly one.

resolution_state_class.description: Account-mapping resolution state. An account switch MUST transition a resolved mapping to stale_after_account_switch; silent resolution under a different actor class is forbidden.

relation_class.description: Frozen relation set between a local Aureline object and its provider-side counterpart. Every provider-linked surface renders exactly one relation class; a surface that cannot place a pair in this matrix routes to cached_read_only_shadow.

drift_state_class.description: Drift state between local and provider sides of a relation. both_moved and actor_scope_changed require user reconciliation before any further mutation.

next_safe_action_class.description: Next-safe-action hint read by queue-review, support-export, and admin-reconciliation consumers. Surfaces MUST render this and MUST NOT invent surface-local actions that fall outside this set.

irreversibility_class.description: Irreversibility class of the predicted side effect. irreversible and irreversible_release_class MUST carry a preview ref.

predicted_side_effect_class.description: Side-effect class reused from the runtime authority-ticket contract so lineage and queue items project onto the same axis.

object_identity.description: Provider-side identity of the object the queue item, mapping, preview, or relation references.

origin_disclosure.description: Canonical Aureline origin a queue item was minted under. Revalidated at drain; callbacks and drains against a mismatched origin are denied.

policy_context.description: Policy context the queue item was minted under. Revalidated at drain; a policy-epoch roll between queue and drain re-evaluates the item.

freshness_floor_ref.description: Opaque ref to the freshness floor the preview was computed against. A queued preview whose freshness floor has drifted is invalidated at drain.

preview_hash.description: Content hash over the structured fields so tampering between preview and spend is detectable. Never a hash of a raw provider body.

provider_consequence_preview_record.description: User-confirmed consequence snapshot a queue item, publish_now ticket, or open_in_provider packet commits to. Queue-review surfaces render the projected_diff_summary and projected_scope_summary verbatim; the user who confirms the drain MUST see the same preview the queue item was admitted under.

resolved_actor_subject.description: May be empty string until resolution_state_class is resolved.

candidate_actor_subject_refs.description: Opaque refs to candidate linked_account_refs / linked_install_refs / linked_delegated_credential_refs on the connected-provider record the user may select between.

superseded_by_account_mapping_ref.description: Opaque ref to the replacement mapping when resolution_state_class is stale_after_account_switch and the user has reselected.

allOf.description: A resolved mapping MUST carry a non-empty resolved_actor_subject.
An unresolved mapping MUST NOT claim a concrete actor class other than unknown_actor_class.

account_mapping_binding_record.description: Bind between the local actor subject, the provider-actor class that will be used on the provider, and the connected-provider record the action runs against. An account switch after queue time MUST transition the mapping to stale_after_account_switch and park the queue item in state awaiting_account_reselection.

originating_local_draft_ref.description: Opaque ref to the local draft object. Required for local_draft and deferred_publish modes; empty string for pure open_in_provider items whose origin is a browser-handoff packet only.

originating_browser_handoff_packet_ref.description: Opaque ref to the browser-handoff packet the queue item parked. Required when mode_admission_reason is browser_handoff_blocked or mutation_mode is open_in_provider.

originating_approval_ticket_ref.description: Opaque ref to the admitting approval ticket. A queued item carries the request for a drain ticket; the drain ticket is minted fresh at drain time with a ticket_lineage entry naming the queued request.

pending_prerequisites.description: Ordered typed prerequisite records. The drain MUST refuse while any required prerequisite is unmet.

consequence_preview_ref.description: Opaque ref to the provider_consequence_preview_record. Required when mutation_mode is publish_now, deferred_publish, or open_in_provider for an irreversible or release-class action; required when conflict_policy_class is force_overwrite_with_preview.

account_mapping_binding_ref.description: Opaque ref to the account_mapping_binding_record the item was queued under. Required whenever the item was queued before the actor class / subject was fully resolved.

last_review_ref.description: Opaque ref to the latest publish_later_queue_review_record.

superseded_by_queue_item_ref.description: Opaque ref to the queue item that superseded this one when queue_state is superseded.

allOf.description: inspect_only items MAY NOT be admitted to the publish-later queue; inspect_only state rides imported snapshots, not queue items.
browser_handoff_blocked admission and open_in_provider mutation_mode MUST cite an originating browser-handoff packet.
local_draft and deferred_publish MUST cite an originating local draft ref.
account_mapping_pending admission MUST cite an account_mapping_binding_ref.
force_overwrite_with_preview conflict policy MUST carry a consequence_preview_ref.

publish_later_queue_item_record.description: Keeps a provider-linked action alive across offline, outage, browser-handoff blocked, status-preview, account-switch, freshness-drift, policy-epoch-roll, and rollback transitions. Drain runs through a fresh approval ticket with action_class deferred_publish_drain; the queue item never carries mutation authority from its queued moment to its published moment.

reviewer_subject_ref.description: Opaque ref to the reviewer's actor subject (user id, admin console seat id, supervisor path). Empty string only when the reviewer actor class is unknown_actor_class for a repair-only record.

audit_event_refs.description: Opaque refs to the provider_handoff audit events emitted for this review (provider_action_deferred, deferred_publish_queue_drained, deferred_publish_queue_rejected).

publish_later_queue_review_record.description: Snapshot of a human or admin review pass over the publish-later queue. Queue-review surfaces, support exports, and admin reconciliation consumers read this to surface the next safe action.

local_ref.description: Opaque ref to the local side (draft, queue item, handoff packet, link record, or imported snapshot).

provider_side_ref.description: Opaque ref to the provider-side object when the relation has one; empty string for local_draft.

target_object_identity.description: Provider-side object identity. Empty only for local_draft relations with no remote counterpart.

last_authoritative_fetch_ref.description: Opaque ref to the most recent import-session record that refreshed the relation. Required for provider_authoritative_object and cached_read_only_shadow.

allOf.description: provider_authoritative_object and cached_read_only_shadow MUST carry a last_authoritative_fetch_ref.
local_draft relations MUST carry an empty provider_side_ref.
Non-local_draft relations MUST carry a non-empty provider_side_ref.

provider_object_relation_record.description: Typed relation between a local Aureline object and its provider-side counterpart. Every provider-linked surface renders exactly one relation class; surfaces MAY NOT invent relation vocabulary outside this record.
